usage: grep [-abcDEFGHhIiJLlMmnOopqRSsUVvwXxZz] [-A num] [-B num] [-C num]
	[-e pattern] [-f file] [--binary-files=value] [--color=when]
	[--context[=num]] [--directories=action] [--label] [--line-buffered]
	[--null] [pattern] [file ...]

  -A num, --after-context=num
          Print num lines of trailing context after each match.
  -B num, --before-context=num
          Print num lines of leading context before each match.
  -C num, --context=num
          Print num lines of leading and trailing context.
  -D action, --devices=action
          Specify the demanded action for devices, FIFOs and sockets.
  -E, --extended-regexp
          Interpret pattern as an extended regular expression.
  -F, --fixed-strings
          Interpret pattern as a set of fixed strings.
  -G, --basic-regexp
          Interpret pattern as a basic regular expression (default).
  -H      Always print filename headers with output lines.
  -I      Ignore binary files.
  -L, --files-without-match
          Only the names of files not containing selected lines are written.
  -R, -r, --recursive
          Recursively search subdirectories listed.
  -V, --version
          Display version information and exit.
  -c, --count
          Only a count of selected lines is written to standard output.
  -e pattern, --regexp=pattern
          Specify a pattern used during the search of the input.
  -i, --ignore-case
          Perform case insensitive matching.
  -l, --files-with-matches
          Only the names of files containing selected lines are written.
  -n, --line-number
          Each output line is preceded by its relative line number.
  -o, --only-matching
          Prints only the matching part of the lines.
  -q, --quiet, --silent
          Quiet mode.
  -v, --invert-match
          Selected lines are those not matching any of the specified patterns.
  -w, --word-regexp
          The expression is searched for as a word.
